The court found significant that the due process claim “has been that the SEC’s procedural rules are unfair light ‘facts and circumstances [their] case'” and not that “the SEC’s rules are unconstitutional every instance.” Slip op.

The numerous grounds asserted show that the respondents had been prevented from presenting fair defense did not provide jurisdiction because they “all are inextricably intertwined with [the] ongoing administrative proceeding and can reviewed court appeals.” Id.

And because the challenges involve the day-to-day conduct the proceeding, the due process arguments are not “wholly collateral” the proceeding under Thunder Basin.

Finally, “plaintiffs fail articulate any convincing reason why the SEC lacks the competence consider the fairness proceedings before its ALJs.”
